title: Increasing Virtual Memory on Windows
breadcrumbs: Development Considerations for Performance and Scalability
text: Increasing Virtual Memory on Windows Uniface can take advantage of extra memory on Windows systems that have 1 GB or more of physical memory available. On 64-bit systems, Uniface can automatically claim up to 4 GB of extra virtual memory. On 32-bit Windows platforms, you need to enable Windows' RAM tuning feature: Set the Windows /3GB startup parameter in the boot.ini file. For example: multi(0)disk(0)rdisk(0)partition(1)/WIN7="Microsoft Windows 7 Professional" /noexecute=optin /fastdetect /3GB Copy Restart the Windows. The /3GB parameter changes the way Windows allocates its 4 GB of virtual address space, enlarging the memory available to user applications to 3 GB, and reducing the memory available to kernel-mode components to the remaining 1 GB. For applications that do not use much memory, this parameter makes no difference, but it can be helpful for applications that often run out of memory (Uniface message).
